items.title: This tool explains binary size
items.oneliner: It maps ELF binary size to functions and inline calls, then renders an interactive HTML report for firmware trimming.

items.title: This leaderboard tests LoRA speed
items.oneliner: It compares fine-tuning methods by verified wall-clock time on GSM8K and SQuAD, so you can benchmark training tricks.

items.title: This server streams Linux games
items.oneliner: It streams PC games to Moonlight clients with isolated sessions, HDR support, and hardware encoding on Linux.

items.title: This guide builds Amiga tooling
items.oneliner: It walks through GCC setup, emulator configuration, running binaries, and remote debugging for AmigaOS development on Linux.

items.title: This post builds Git CI
items.oneliner: It shows how to wire post-receive hooks and nq into a tiny self-hosted CI pipeline with logs.

items.title: This addon reviews patches in email
items.oneliner: It lets Thunderbird users comment on git patches, reply to mailing lists, and apply reviewed patches locally.

items.title: This CLI controls a smart bed
items.oneliner: It exposes a usable API client for Sleep.me hardware, so you can script bed temperature from the terminal.

items.title: This tool self-hosts Git securely
items.oneliner: It adds fine-grained access control, repo management, and automation hooks for teams running Git on their own server.

items.title: This post removes Go bounds checks
items.oneliner: It shows when unsafe can eliminate Go bounds checks, with assembly-level examples for speeding hot loops.

items.title: This project builds a Haskell blog
items.oneliner: It gives you a forkable static blog generator in Shake with posts, tags, pages, and GitHub Pages hosting.
